summary: Verified homepage bug fix (pc?.about_description optional chaining). Homepage loads successfully with all sections rendered: hero slider (full-width image, no blue overlay), stats cards, About IDSEA, Membership Types (4 plans), Upcoming Events, Latest News, Our Growing Community (48+ members with category breakdown), and CTA. No JS errors on page load, no TypeError from null pc access.

issues: 'Dairy Science' about image appears to render as broken image / alt text only in the About section - pc.about_image URL may be missing or failing to load. Fallback unsplash URL should render but did not appear during test. Low priority - cosmetic only, homepage renders fine.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Investigate why About section image shows broken image icon / alt text 'Dairy Science' - could be a CORS/network issue with the unsplash fallback OR pc.about_image returning an invalid URL. Not blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: HomePage.jsx uses many inline styles - could be refactored to CSS modules or styled components for maintainability (not blocking)
News section renders raw HTML via dangerouslySetInnerHTML for item.content - ensure backend sanitizes user input to prevent XSS
context_for_next_testing_agent: Homepage bug (blank page due to TypeError on pc.about_description) has been fixed and verified. All homepage sections render correctly. Member stats show real DB counts: total 48+, Academic 31, Entrepreneur 12, Corporate 4, International 1. Hero slider displays full-width image without blue overlay. Only minor cosmetic issue: About section fallback image may not load consistently.
rca of the issue: Root cause was pc state initialized to null but two dangerouslySetInnerHTML lines at HomePage.jsx:205-206 accessed pc.about_description and pc.about_description2 without optional chaining. When API responded before render (or when API failed), pc was null and accessing .about_description on null caused TypeError → white screen. Fix: changed to pc?.about_description and pc?.about_description2. Verified via browser automation: page renders fully without errors, all sections load, no console/page errors detected.
